_description: ESPN team/player stats for Argentina (3 group games) and Cape Verde (3 group games), R32 ARG vs CPV 2026-07-03

notes: Perfect group record (3W, 9pts), scored 8 goals conceded 1. Clear best team of the tournament so far.
Messi scored 6 of Argentina's 8 group goals: hat-trick vs Algeria (17',60',76'), brace vs Austria (38',90+5'), 1 free-kick vs Jordan (80', coming off the bench).
Other scorers: Lo Celso (free kick vs Jordan), Lautaro Martinez (penalty vs Jordan) -- Lautaro's ONLY goal in 3 games, and it was a penalty (0 open-play goals).
Julian Alvarez: 0 goals, 0 assists in 3 group games; benched (sub appearance only) in 2 of 3.
Messi SOT by game: 4, 4, 1 (avg 3.0); shots: 6, 7, 2. Extreme current form (6 goals) despite this being just group stage.

Cinderella story: unbeaten in group stage (3 draws, 3pts), advanced as a best-third-place team. Extremely resilient defense -- clean sheets vs Spain (survived 74% possession, 27 shots) and Saudi Arabia.

Most analogous opponent-quality game to today (elite possession/press side) is the Spain match: CPV managed only 6 shots, 1 SOT, 25.7% possession -- the single most relevant precedent for how they'll look against Argentina.
Their best attacking output (12 shots, 4 SOT) came in their most open game (vs Uruguay, 2-2), not against elite opposition.
Low-foul, low-card team generally (1 foul(!) vs Spain -- extremely passive/deep defensive setup, not a disciplinary risk in the traditional sense).
